##
## Dev Environment - Main Configuration
## Author: Raul Peña (rpena@shokworks.io)
## Purpose: Orchestrates all infrastructure modules for the development environment
##
## Environment: Development (cost-optimized, single-region)
##
## Modules & Resources Deployed:
## - Keycloak: Authentication and identity management


##=============================================================================
## CURRENT AZURE CONTEXT
##=============================================================================
## Get current subscription and tenant information for identity and RBAC configuration

data "azurerm_client_config" "current" {}


##=============================================================================
## DATA: KEYCLOAK AUTHENTICATION FLOW - DEFAULT BROWSER
##=============================================================================
## Fetches the built-in default "browser" authentication flow.
## This flow is the standard method used when users authenticate via a web browser.
## It is used as a base to retrieve and modify specific execution steps within it.
##

data "keycloak_authentication_flow" "default_browser" {

  realm_id = keycloak_realm.main.id
  alias    = "browser"
}


##=============================================================================
## DATA: KEYCLOAK AUTHENTICATION EXECUTION - DEFAULT IDP REDIRECTOR
##=============================================================================
## Fetches the built-in "identity-provider-redirector" execution step from the 
## default browser flow. This reference is required to override its configuration
## (e.g., configuring automatic redirection to the legacy IdP).
##

data "keycloak_authentication_execution" "default_idp_redirector" {

  realm_id          = keycloak_realm.main.id
  parent_flow_alias = data.keycloak_authentication_flow.default_browser.alias
  provider_id       = "identity-provider-redirector"
}


##=============================================================================
## KEYCLOAK RESOURCES
##=============================================================================

##=============================================================================
## KEYCLOAK REALM - MAIN
##=============================================================================
## Creates the primary realm for Lexxi Keycloak authentication
## Realms are isolated authentication domains where users, clients, and roles exist
##

resource "keycloak_realm" "main" {

  realm        = "IntellxxDNA"
  enabled      = true
  display_name = "IntellxxDNA Realm"

  # Password policy: minimum 12 chars, 1 uppercase, 1 lowercase, 1 digit, 1 special char
  password_policy = "length(12) and upperCase(1) and lowerCase(1) and digits(1) and specialChars(1)"
}


##=============================================================================
## KEYCLOAK OPENID CLIENT - IXXD LEGACY
##=============================================================================
## Creates an OpenID Connect (OIDC) confidential client for the Legacy application.
## This client is used to integrate the existing .NET application with Keycloak
## for centralized authentication and single sign-on (SSO).
##
## Features:
## - Confidential client (requires a client secret)
## - Standard flow (Authorization Code Flow) enabled
## - Strict redirect URIs and web origins targeting the legacy app endpoint
##

resource "keycloak_openid_client" "ixxd-legacy" {

  access_type                                = "CONFIDENTIAL"
  backchannel_logout_revoke_offline_sessions = false
  backchannel_logout_session_required        = true
  backchannel_logout_url                     = null
  client_authenticator_type                  = "client-secret"
  client_id                                  = "ixxd-legacy"
  client_secret                              = null # sensitive
  consent_required                           = false
  description                                = "IXXD Legacy Application"
  direct_access_grants_enabled               = false
  display_on_consent_screen                  = false
  enabled                                    = true
  extra_config                               = {}
  frontchannel_logout_enabled                = true
  frontchannel_logout_url                    = null
  full_scope_allowed                         = true
  implicit_flow_enabled                      = false
  import                                     = false
  login_theme                                = null
  name                                       = "IXXD Legacy"
  oauth2_device_authorization_grant_enabled  = false
  oauth2_device_code_lifespan                = null
  oauth2_device_polling_interval             = null
  pkce_code_challenge_method                 = null
  realm_id                                   = keycloak_realm.main.id
  root_url                                   = var.ixxd_legacy_client_root_url
  service_accounts_enabled                   = false
  standard_flow_enabled                      = true
  use_refresh_tokens                         = true
  use_refresh_tokens_client_credentials      = false
  valid_post_logout_redirect_uris            = ["${var.legacy_dotnet_idp_base_url}/logout*", "${var.ixxd_legacy_client_root_url}*"]
  valid_redirect_uris                        = ["", "${var.ixxd_legacy_client_root_url}*"]
  web_origins                                = ["+"]
}


##=============================================================================
## KEYCLOAK OPENID CLIENT - LEXXI API
##=============================================================================
## Creates an OpenID Connect (OIDC) confidential client for the Lexxi API.
## This client provides secure authentication for backend API services, allowing
## validation of tokens issued by Keycloak and enabling machine-to-machine or
## direct access flows if necessary.
##
## Features:
## - Confidential client (requires a client secret)
## - Standard flow (Authorization Code Flow) and Direct Access Grants enabled
## - Supports wildcard redirect URIs and web origins (typical for dev environments)
## - Refresh tokens are disabled for this specific client
##

resource "keycloak_openid_client" "lexi_api" {

  access_type                                = "CONFIDENTIAL"
  backchannel_logout_revoke_offline_sessions = false
  backchannel_logout_session_required        = true
  backchannel_logout_url                     = null
  client_authenticator_type                  = "client-secret"
  client_id                                  = "lexi_api"
  client_secret                              = null # sensitive
  consent_required                           = false
  direct_access_grants_enabled               = true
  display_on_consent_screen                  = false
  enabled                                    = true
  extra_config                               = {}
  frontchannel_logout_enabled                = true
  frontchannel_logout_url                    = null
  full_scope_allowed                         = true
  implicit_flow_enabled                      = false
  import                                     = false
  login_theme                                = null
  oauth2_device_authorization_grant_enabled  = false
  oauth2_device_code_lifespan                = null
  oauth2_device_polling_interval             = null
  pkce_code_challenge_method                 = null
  realm_id                                   = keycloak_realm.main.id
  service_accounts_enabled                   = false
  standard_flow_enabled                      = true
  use_refresh_tokens                         = false
  use_refresh_tokens_client_credentials      = false
  valid_post_logout_redirect_uris            = ["+"]
  valid_redirect_uris                        = ["/*"]
  web_origins                                = ["/*"]
}


##=============================================================================
## KEYCLOAK AUTH EXECUTION CONFIG - IDP AUTO REDIRECT
##=============================================================================
## Configures the "identity-provider-redirector" step in the default browser 
## authentication flow to automatically redirect users to the external Identity 
## Provider (legacy-dotnet). 
##
## Features:
## - Bypasses the standard Keycloak login screen for a seamless SSO experience
## - Automatically routes unauthenticated users directly to the legacy .NET login
##

resource "keycloak_authentication_execution_config" "idp_auto_redirect" {

  realm_id     = keycloak_realm.main.id
  execution_id = data.keycloak_authentication_execution.default_idp_redirector.id
  alias        = "auth-api-redirect"

  config = {
    defaultProvider = "legacy-dotnet"
  }
}


##=============================================================================
## KEYCLOAK OIDC IDENTITY PROVIDER - LEGACY DOTNET
##=============================================================================
## Configures the legacy .NET application as an external Identity Provider (IdP)
## in Keycloak via OpenID Connect (OIDC). This allows users to authenticate
## using their existing legacy credentials, with Keycloak acting as an identity broker.
##
## Features:
## - Acts as an OIDC identity broker for the legacy app
## - Maps to the authorization and token endpoints of the .NET application
## - Supports backchannel logout integration
## - Sync mode set to IMPORT to sync user identities into Keycloak upon login
##

resource "keycloak_oidc_identity_provider" "legacy-dotnet" {

  accepts_prompt_none_forward_from_client = null
  add_read_token_role_on_create           = false
  alias                                   = "legacy-dotnet"
  authenticate_by_default                 = false
  authorization_url                       = "${var.legacy_dotnet_idp_base_url}/connect/authorize"
  backchannel_supported                   = true
  client_id                               = "keycloak-broker"
  client_secret                           = var.legacy_dotnet_idp_client_secret
  default_scopes                          = null
  disable_user_info                       = false
  display_name                            = "Log in with Legacy"
  enabled                                 = true
  extra_config                            = {}
  first_broker_login_flow_alias           = null
  gui_order                               = null
  hide_on_login_page                      = false
  issuer                                  = null
  jwks_url                                = null
  link_only                               = false
  login_hint                              = "false"
  logout_url                              = "${var.legacy_dotnet_idp_base_url}/backchannel-logout"
  post_broker_login_flow_alias            = null
  provider_id                             = null
  realm                                   = keycloak_realm.main.id
  store_token                             = false
  sync_mode                               = "IMPORT"
  token_url                               = "${var.legacy_dotnet_idp_base_url}/connect/token"
  trust_email                             = false
  ui_locales                              = false
  user_info_url                           = null
  validate_signature                      = false
}










































##=============================================================================
## KEYCLOAK OPENID CLIENT - LEXXI WEB APP
##=============================================================================
## Registers the Lexxi frontend as a public OAuth2/OpenID Connect client
## This client handles user authentication for the Lexxi single-page application
##
## Client Type: PUBLIC
## Authentication Flow: Authorization Code Flow with PKCE (OAuth 2.0 standard)
## Purpose: Secure browser-based authentication without exposing client secrets
##
## Security Notes:
## - Public clients cannot securely store secrets (runs in user's browser)
## - PKCE (RFC 7636) prevents authorization code interception attacks
## - Redirect URIs must be explicitly whitelisted to prevent open redirects
## - CORS origins limited to prevent unauthorized token requests
##

resource "keycloak_openid_client" "lexxi_web_app_client" {

  realm_id  = keycloak_realm.main.id
  client_id = "lexxi-web-app-client"
  name      = "Lexxi Web App Client"
  enabled   = true

  ##=======================================================================
  ## CLIENT AUTHENTICATION & FLOW SETTINGS
  ##=======================================================================
  ## access_type = "PUBLIC"
  ##   - Indicates this is a public client (browser-based SPA)
  ##   - Client cannot authenticate with a secret
  ##   - Used for single-page applications, mobile apps, desktop clients
  ##
  ## standard_flow_enabled = true
  ##   - Enables Authorization Code Flow (recommended for SPAs)
  ##   - User redirects to Keycloak login → receives auth code → app exchanges for token
  ##   - More secure than implicit flow (flow no longer recommended)
  ##
  access_type           = "PUBLIC"
  standard_flow_enabled = true

  ##=======================================================================
  ## PKCE (PROOF KEY FOR AUTHORIZATION CODE EXCHANGE)
  ##=======================================================================
  ## pkce_code_challenge_method = "S256"
  ##   - PKCE adds extra security layer to Authorization Code Flow
  ##   - Prevents authorization code interception/replay attacks
  ##   - Client generates random code_verifier → sends code_challenge (SHA256 hash)
  ##   - When exchanging code for token, client proves it has original code_verifier
  ##   - Mandatory security requirement for public clients in production (2026+ standards)
  ##   - Options: "S256" (SHA256, recommended) or "plain" (not recommended)
  ##
  pkce_code_challenge_method = "S256"

  ##=======================================================================
  ## REDIRECT URIS (Authorization Code Response Destinations)
  ##=======================================================================
  ## valid_redirect_uris = [...]
  ##   - Whitelisted URLs where Keycloak can redirect after user login
  ##   - Prevents open redirect attacks (attacker cannot redirect to arbitrary URLs)
  ##   - Must match exactly (protocol, domain, path) - wildcards allowed only on paths
  ##   - Azure Static Web App: automatically generates *.azurestaticapps.net domain
  ##   - Wildcard pattern: "https://domain/*" allows all paths under domain
  ##
  ##   Example redirects that will be allowed:
  ##   - https://myapp.azurestaticapps.net/
  ##   - https://myapp.azurestaticapps.net/dashboard
  ##   - https://myapp.azurestaticapps.net/auth/callback
  ##
  valid_redirect_uris = [
    "https://localhost/*"
  ]

  ##=======================================================================
  ## WEB ORIGINS (CORS - Cross-Origin Resource Sharing)
  ##=======================================================================
  ## web_origins = [...]
  ##   - Whitelisted origins allowed to make CORS requests to Keycloak
  ##   - Prevents malicious websites from stealing user tokens
  ##   - Only allows JavaScript from known origins to access Keycloak tokens
  ##   - Must omit path (CORS is enforced at origin level, not path level)
  ##   - Typically same as your frontend domain
  ##
  ##   Security Impact:
  ##   - Without CORS restriction: Any website can make requests to Keycloak on user's behalf
  ##   - With CORS restriction: Only your frontend domain can access user tokens/endpoints
  ##
  web_origins = [
    "https://localhost"
  ]

  depends_on = [
    keycloak_realm.main
  ]
}

##=============================================================================
## KEYCLOAK OPENID CLIENT - LEXXI BACKEND API
##=============================================================================
## Registers the Lexxi backend API as a confidential OAuth2/OpenID Connect client
## This client represents server-to-server or internal API communication
##
## Client Type: CONFIDENTIAL (Backend API)
## Authentication: Client credentials flow (for service-to-service auth)
## Purpose: Secure API-to-API communication with client secret authentication
##
## Security Notes:
## - Confidential clients can securely store secrets (runs on secure server)
## - Uses client_id + client_secret for authentication (like Basic Auth)
## - Service accounts enable service-to-service authentication
## - Tokens include 'aud' (audience) claim to restrict token usage to specific APIs
##
## Communication Pattern:
## 1. Lexxi API → Keycloak: POST /token with client_id + client_secret
## 2. Keycloak → Lexxi API: Returns JWT with audience claim
## 3. Frontend/other services: Can verify token is meant for this API via 'aud' claim
##

resource "keycloak_openid_client" "lexxi_backend_api" {

  realm_id  = keycloak_realm.main.id
  client_id = "lexxi-backend-api-client"
  name      = "Lexxi Backend API Client"
  enabled   = true

  ##=======================================================================
  ## CLIENT AUTHENTICATION SETTINGS
  ##=======================================================================
  ## access_type = "CONFIDENTIAL"
  ##   - Indicates this is a confidential client (backend server)
  ##   - Client can securely store and use a secret
  ##   - Uses client credentials or authorization code + secret for auth
  ##
  ## service_accounts_enabled = true
  ##   - Allows API to authenticate on its own (service-to-service auth)
  ##   - Without this: API can only be called with user-delegated tokens
  ##   - With this: API can obtain tokens directly for internal operations
  ##   - Use case: Batch jobs, scheduled tasks, inter-service calls
  ##
  access_type              = "CONFIDENTIAL"
  service_accounts_enabled = true

  depends_on = [
    keycloak_openid_client.lexxi_web_app_client
  ]
}


##=============================================================================
## KEYCLOAK OPENID AUDIENCE PROTOCOL MAPPER
##=============================================================================
## Adds the 'aud' (Audience) claim to JWT tokens for the Python API client
## Audience claim restricts token usage to specific intended recipients
##
## What is the 'aud' Claim?
## ├─ Standard OAuth2/OpenID Connect claim in JWT tokens
## ├─ Specifies which API/service is intended to consume the token
## ├─ Prevents token misuse (stolen token can't be used for wrong API)
## └─ Example JWT payload: { aud: "python-backend-api", sub: "user123" }
##
## Security Flow:
## 1. Frontend gets token: { aud: "python-backend-api", sub: "user123" }
## 2. Frontend sends token to Python API
## 3. Python API verifies: aud claim == "python-backend-api" ✓
## 4. Token accepted and processed
## 5. If attacker tries same token on different API:
##    - That API checks: aud claim != "my-api" ✗
##    - Token rejected (prevents cross-API token reuse)
##
## Mapper Type: aud (Audience) Claim Mapper
## Maps to: Frontend client (angular_client) - specifies which client gets this mapper
## Audience value: "lexxi-backend-api" - the target API identifier
##




##=============================================================================
## KEYCLOAK OPENID AUDIENCE PROTOCOL MAPPER - LEXXI BACKEND API
##=============================================================================
## Injects the 'aud' (Audience) claim into JWT tokens issued to the Lexxi
## Web App client, so the Lexxi Backend API can verify the token was intended
## specifically for it.
##
## Why is this needed?
## ├─ Without an audience claim, a token issued for any client could technically
## │  be sent to any API — nothing stops token reuse across services
## ├─ With this mapper, every token issued to the frontend includes:
## │  "aud": "lexxi-backend-api"
## └─ The Backend API validates this claim and rejects tokens meant for others
##
## Where it is attached:
## ├─ Client: lexxi-web-app-client (the Angular SPA)
## └─ Effect: every token the frontend obtains will carry the audience claim
##
## Security Flow:
## 1. User logs in via the Angular SPA → Keycloak issues JWT
## 2. JWT contains: { "aud": "lexxi-backend-api", "sub": "user-uuid", ... }
## 3. Angular sends JWT to the Backend API
## 4. Backend API checks: aud == "lexxi-backend-api" ✓ → request accepted
## 5. If same token is sent to a different API:
##    └─ That API checks: aud != "its-own-id" ✗ → request rejected
##
## Mapper Type: Audience (aud claim)
## Attached to: lexxi-web-app-client
## Audience value injected: "lexxi-backend-api"
##

resource "keycloak_openid_audience_protocol_mapper" "api_audience_mapper" {

  realm_id  = keycloak_realm.main.id
  client_id = keycloak_openid_client.lexxi_web_app_client.id
  name      = "lexxi-backend-api-audience-mapper"

  ##=======================================================================
  ## AUDIENCE CONFIGURATION
  ##=======================================================================
  ## included_custom_audience = "lexxi-backend-api"
  ##   - The audience value to include in JWT tokens
  ##   - Must match the Lexxi API's expected audience
  ##   - When frontend requests token, it will include this audience claim
  ##   - Lexxi API can then verify the token is intended for it
  ##
  ##   Example JWT:
  ##   {
  ##     "iss": "https://keycloak.example.com/auth/realms/lexxi",
  ##     "sub": "user-uuid",
  ##     "aud": "lexxi-backend-api",     ← This claim was added by mapper
  ##     "iat": 1609459200,
  ##     "exp": 1609462800
  ##   }
  ##
  included_custom_audience = "lexxi-backend-api"

  depends_on = [
    keycloak_openid_client.lexxi_web_app_client,
    keycloak_openid_client.lexxi_backend_api
  ]
}

##
## Dev Environment - Input Variables
## Author: Raul Peña (rpena@shokworks.io)
##
## Purpose: Defines configurable parameters for the development environment
## These variables can be overridden via:
## - Command line: terraform apply -var="key=value"
## - .tfvars files: terraform apply -var-file="dev.tfvars"
## - Environment variables: TF_VAR_key=value
##
## Current defaults are optimized for development cost and simplicity
##

##=============================================================================
## KEYCLOAK CONFIGURATION
##=============================================================================
## Identity and Access Management (IAM) service configuration
## Keycloak provides OIDC/OAuth2, LDAP/AD integration, and user management
##

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Variable: keycloak_url
# Description: Defines the base endpoint for the Keycloak API.
# Used by the Keycloak Terraform provider for authentication and resource management.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
variable "keycloak_url" {
  description = "The base URL for the Keycloak instance (e.g., https://api-dev.ixxdna.com/auth). This endpoint is used by the Terraform provider to authenticate and manage Keycloak resources."
  type        = string
  sensitive   = false
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Variable: keycloak_client_id
# Description: The OIDC/OAuth2 Client ID for the Keycloak Terraform provider.
# Must have adequate permissions to manage realm configurations.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
variable "keycloak_client_id" {
  description = "The Keycloak client ID used by the Terraform provider to authenticate via OIDC/OAuth2. Usually defaults to 'admin-cli' for administrative access."
  type        = string
  default     = "admin-cli"
  sensitive   = false
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Variable: keycloak_admin_username
# Description: Administrative username for Keycloak access.
# Required by the provider to authenticate via direct grant or similar flow.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
variable "keycloak_admin_username" {
  description = "The username for the Keycloak administrative console. This user must have sufficient privileges to configure realms, clients, and identity providers."
  type        = string
  sensitive   = false
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Variable: keycloak_admin_password
# Description: Administrative password for Keycloak access.
# This variable is marked as sensitive to prevent leakage in Terraform logs.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
variable "keycloak_admin_password" {
  description = "The password for the Keycloak administrative console. Treated as sensitive to prevent it from being output in plaintext."
  type        = string
  sensitive   = true

  validation {
    condition     = length(var.keycloak_admin_password) >= 8
    error_message = "Keycloak admin password must be at least 8 characters long."
  }
}

##=============================================================================
## LEGACY DOTNET IDP & CLIENT CONFIGURATION
##=============================================================================

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Variable: legacy_dotnet_idp_base_url
# Description: Base URL for the legacy .NET application acting as an OIDC IdP.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
variable "legacy_dotnet_idp_base_url" {
  description = "Base URL for the legacy .NET application acting as an OIDC IdP (e.g., https://10.2.1.6:50533)"
  type        = string
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Variable: legacy_dotnet_idp_client_secret
# Description: Client secret used by Keycloak to authenticate against the legacy IdP.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
variable "legacy_dotnet_idp_client_secret" {
  description = "Client secret used by Keycloak to authenticate against the legacy IdP."
  type        = string
  sensitive   = true
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Variable: ixxd_legacy_client_root_url
# Description: Root URL for the legacy .NET application client.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
variable "ixxd_legacy_client_root_url" {
  description = "Root URL for the legacy .NET application client (e.g., https://10.2.1.6/)"
  type        = string
}

file.content:
##
## Azure Static Web App Module - Main Configuration
## Author: Raul Peña (rpena@shokworks.io)
##
## Purpose: Creates Azure Static Web App for hosting Angular/React/Vue frontend
## Configuration:
## - SKU: Free tier (perfect for dev/staging)
## - GitHub Integration: Automated CI/CD from GitHub
## - Build: Runs ng build, outputs to dist/
## - Environment Variables: API endpoint and custom config
##
## Features:
## - Free HTTPS with Azure-managed certificate
## - Global CDN distribution
## - GitHub Actions workflow (auto-generated)
## - Staging environments for PR testing
## - Custom domain support
##

##=============================================================================
## STATIC WEB APP
##=============================================================================
## Core Static Web App resource for hosting the Angular frontend

resource "azurerm_static_web_app" "app" {

  name                = "${var.resource_prefix}-${var.app_name}"
  location            = var.location
  resource_group_name = var.resource_group_name
  sku_tier            = var.sku_tier
  sku_size            = var.sku_size
  tags                = var.tags
}


##=============================================================================
## ENVIRONMENT CONFIGURATION
##=============================================================================
## Stores API endpoint and other configuration as app settings

locals {
  app_settings = merge(
    {
      "API_ENDPOINT" = var.api_endpoint
    },
    var.api_key != "" ? { "API_KEY" = var.api_key } : {},
    var.environment_variables
  )
}


# All outputs are defined in outputs.tf to keep module organization clean

content.thinking:
OK, so the Static Web App module stores `API_ENDPOINT` as an environment variable. In prod, this is set to `module.front_door.endpoint_fqdn` - this is the Azure Front Door endpoint FQDN (something like `intellectxxdna-prod-fd-endpoint.azurefd.net`), NOT `api.ixxdna.com`.

So the frontend JavaScript would call `https://<afd-endpoint>.azurefd.net/rd/user/change-password`, not `https://api.ixxdna.com/rd/user/change-password`!


When the frontend at `https://rnd.ixxdna.com` makes a request to the AFD endpoint, it sends `Origin: https://rnd.ixxdna.com`, and since that origin is in the `CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS` list, the backend should return the matching `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` header. But I need to verify whether the browser actually permits this cross-origin request in the first place.

Now I'm realizing the issue might be about which domain the frontend is actually calling. The `api.ixxdna.com` custom domain points to the same AFD endpoint as the default FQDN, but the `API_ENDPOINT` configuration uses the default AFD domain, not the custom domain. However, the user mentioned hitting `https://api.ixxdna.com/rd/user/change-password`, which suggests either the frontend is configured differently than expected, or there's a mismatch between what domain is being used and what's configured.

Rather than getting lost in the configuration details, the real problem is clear: the production CORS allowlist only has two origins configured, so any request from a different domain would fail. That's likely the root cause here. or even `https://lexxi.ixxdna.com` if the Lexxi frontend calls the RD API), it would fail.

3. **The addons/prod main.tf has `lexxi_web_app_client` with `valid_redirect_uris = ["https://localhost/*"]` and `web_origins = ["https://localhost"]`.** This is clearly not configured for prod.

4. **Azure Front Door doesn't have CORS header preservation configured.** There's no rule set to preserve or properly handle CORS headers.

5. **Most importantly: the `change-password` endpoint might be called from the Keycloak account management page** at `https://api.ixxdna.com/auth/...`, but that would be same-origin anyway.

Let me reconsider this. The user is hitting `https://api.ixxdna.com/rd/user/change-password` — what if they're actually accessing it from the Lexxi app at `https://lexxi.ixxdna.com`? That origin is in the `rd_cors_allow_origins` list, so it should work. But why would Lexxi even call the RD API for a password change? Or maybe the frontend is using the Azure Front Door FQDN as the API endpoint through an environment variable, and the actual origin depends on how the user is accessing the app.

The real issue might be that the container hasn't been redeployed with the latest Terraform changes — the code shows the correct CORS origins configured, but if the container was deployed before these settings were applied or there's a key vault caching issue, the running instance could have different or missing CORS values.

Looking at the broader picture, the most likely culprit is that Azure Front Door Standard's caching behavior is stripping the `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` header from responses. When a cached response is served to a request from a different origin, the CDN node won't include the CORS header even if it was originally present, which explains why the frontend at `https://rnd.ixxdna.com` (or `https://lexxi.ixxdna.com`) can't access the API through the Front Door endpoint.

The most straightforward fix would be to add an Azure Front Door Rule Set that explicitly injects the correct CORS response headers, ensuring they're always present regardless of caching behavior.

##

module "static_web_app_rd" {

  source = "../../modules/static-web-app"

  resource_group_name = azurerm_resource_group.rg.name
  location            = var.azure_region
  environment         = var.environment
  resource_prefix     = local.resource_prefix
  app_name            = "rd"

  # GitHub Configuration
  github_repo_url = var.rd_github_repo_url
  github_token    = var.github_token
  github_branch   = var.rd_github_branch

  # Build Configuration
  app_location    = var.swa_app_location
  output_location = var.swa_output_location
  build_command   = var.swa_build_command

  # API Configuration
  api_endpoint = module.front_door.endpoint_fqdn
  api_key      = ""

  sku_tier = "Free"
  sku_size = "Free"
  tags     = local.tags

  depends_on = [
    module.front_door,
    azurerm_container_app.rd
  ]
}

## STATIC WEB APP CUSTOM DOMAIN for rnd
resource "azurerm_static_web_app_custom_domain" "rnd_prod" {

  count             = var.enable_custom_domain_to_rd ? 1 : 0
  static_web_app_id = module.static_web_app_rd.static_web_app_id
  domain_name       = "rnd.ixxdna.com"
  validation_type   = "cname-delegation"
}

file.content:
module "front_door" {

  source = "../../modules/front-door"

  resource_group_name = azurerm_resource_group.rg.name
  environment         = var.environment
  resource_prefix     = local.resource_prefix
  tags                = local.tags

  # Front Door Configuration
  front_door_sku          = var.front_door_sku
  custom_domain_host_name = "api-dev.ixxdna.com"

  # Backend Configuration - Multiple backends for Lexxi, RD, and Keycloak with path-based routing
  # NOTE: Route Keycloak over HTTPS to bypass HTTP-only proxy header issues
  backends = [
    {
      name              = "lexxi"
      fqdn              = azurerm_container_app.lexxi.ingress[0].fqdn
      port              = 80
      protocol          = "Http"
      path              = "/lexxi/*"
      health_path       = "/"
      strip_path_prefix = true
      strip_order       = 1
    },
    {
      name              = "rd"
      fqdn              = azurerm_container_app.rd.ingress[0].fqdn
      port              = 80
      protocol          = "Http"
      path              = "/rd/*"
      health_path       = "/"
      strip_path_prefix = true
      strip_order       = 3
    },
    {
      name             = "keycloak"
      fqdn             = azurerm_container_app.keycloak.ingress[0].fqdn
      port             = 443
      protocol         = "Https"
      path             = "/auth/*"
      health_path      = "/auth/realms/master/.well-known/openid-configuration"
      session_affinity = true
    },
    {
      name              = "mlflow"
      fqdn              = azurerm_container_app.mlflow.ingress[0].fqdn
      port              = 80
      protocol          = "Http"
      path              = "/mlflow/*"
      health_path       = "/"
      strip_path_prefix = true
      strip_order       = 2
    }
  ]

  # Diagnostics
  log_analytics_workspace_id = module.storage.log_analytics_workspace_id

  # WAF Configuration
  enable_waf = var.enable_front_door_waf
  waf_mode   = var.front_door_waf_mode

  depends_on = [
    azurerm_container_app.lexxi,
    azurerm_container_app.rd,
    azurerm_container_app.keycloak,
    azurerm_container_app.mlflow,
    module.networking

Now I have all the information I need. Let me implement the fix:

1. Add `cors_allow_origins` variable to the Front Door module
2. Add CORS rule set and rule in the Front Door module main.tf
3. Update the route to include the CORS rule set
4. Update prod main.tf to pass the cors_allow_origins
5. Update dev main.tf to pass the cors_allow_origins

The key change in the Front Door module:
- Add a rule set that, when the `Origin` header matches one of the allowed origins, injects `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: {http_request_header(Origin)}`, `Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true`, and `Vary: Origin` into the response.


- The rule will apply to all responses including OPTIONS preflight requests, which is fine since the backend already handles the preflight and we're just overwriting the CORS headers consistently.

Now I'm implementing the Azure Front Door rule. I need to verify that AFD Standard supports server variables like `{http_request_header(Origin)}` in response header actions so I can dynamically echo back the request origin. Let me check the Azure documentation on what server variables are available in the Rule Engine.

input.old_string:
##=============================================================================
## ROUTES (PATH-BASED ROUTING TO DIFFERENT BACKENDS)
##=============================================================================

resource "azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_route" "backend_route" {

  for_each = local.backends_map

  name                          = "${var.resource_prefix}-${each.value.name}-route"
  cdn_frontdoor_endpoint_id     = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_endpoint.fd_endpoint.id
  cdn_frontdoor_origin_group_id = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_origin_group.backend_group[each.value.name].id
  cdn_frontdoor_origin_ids      = [azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_origin.backend_origin[each.value.name].id]
  cdn_frontdoor_origin_path     = each.value.name == "keycloak" ? null : "/"
  cdn_frontdoor_custom_domain_ids = var.custom_domain_host_name != null ? [azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_custom_domain.custom_domain[0].id] : []
  cdn_frontdoor_rule_set_ids    = each.value.strip_path_prefix ? [azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_rule_set.strip_prefix_rules[0].id] : null
  enabled = true

  # Use HTTP for internal backends (unless specifically HTTPS)
  forwarding_protocol = each.value.protocol == "Https" ? "HttpsOnly" : "HttpOnly"
  patterns_to_match   = distinct([each.value.path, trimsuffix(each.value.path, "/*")])
  supported_protocols = ["Http", "Https"]
}
input.new_string:
##=============================================================================
## CORS RULE SET
##=============================================================================
## When cors_allow_origins is non-empty, this rule set injects the correct
## Access-Control-Allow-Origin and Access-Control-Allow-Credentials headers
## into every response whose Origin matches one of the allowed values.
##
## Why this is needed:
## Azure Front Door Standard CDN can strip or fail to forward CORS response
## headers from the backend, especially for credentialed requests
## (credentials: 'include'). Without Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true
## in the response, browsers reject the response even when the server returned
## HTTP 200 and the operation completed successfully.
##
## The rule uses the AFD server variable {http_request_header(Origin)} to
## echo back the exact request Origin, satisfying the browser requirement
## that Access-Control-Allow-Origin must be a specific origin (not '*') when
## credentials are included.
##=============================================================================

resource "azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_rule_set" "cors_rules" {
  count                    = length(var.cors_allow_origins) > 0 ? 1 : 0
  name                     = "${replace(var.resource_prefix, "-", "")}corsrules"
  cdn_frontdoor_profile_id = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_profile.fd_profile.id
}

resource "azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_rule" "cors_headers" {
  count                     = length(var.cors_allow_origins) > 0 ? 1 : 0
  name                      = "CorsHeaders"
  cdn_frontdoor_rule_set_id = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_rule_set.cors_rules[0].id
  order                     = 1
  behavior_on_match         = "Continue"

  conditions {
    request_header_condition {
      header_name      = "Origin"
      operator         = "Equal"
      negate_condition = false
      transforms       = []
      match_values     = var.cors_allow_origins
    }
  }

  actions {
    response_header_action {
      header_action = "Overwrite"
      header_name   = "Access-Control-Allow-Origin"
      value         = "{http_request_header(Origin)}"
    }
    response_header_action {
      header_action = "Overwrite"
      header_name   = "Access-Control-Allow-Credentials"
      value         = "true"
    }
    response_header_action {
      header_action = "Overwrite"
      header_name   = "Vary"
      value         = "Origin"
    }
  }

  depends_on = [azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_rule_set.cors_rules]
}


##=============================================================================
## ROUTES (PATH-BASED ROUTING TO DIFFERENT BACKENDS)
##=============================================================================

locals {
  has_cors_rules = length(var.cors_allow_origins) > 0
}

resource "azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_route" "backend_route" {

  for_each = local.backends_map

  name                          = "${var.resource_prefix}-${each.value.name}-route"
  cdn_frontdoor_endpoint_id     = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_endpoint.fd_endpoint.id
  cdn_frontdoor_origin_group_id = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_origin_group.backend_group[each.value.name].id
  cdn_frontdoor_origin_ids      = [azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_origin.backend_origin[each.value.name].id]
  cdn_frontdoor_origin_path     = each.value.name == "keycloak" ? null : "/"
  cdn_frontdoor_custom_domain_ids = var.custom_domain_host_name != null ? [azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_custom_domain.custom_domain[0].id] : []
  cdn_frontdoor_rule_set_ids    = compact(concat(
    each.value.strip_path_prefix ? [azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_rule_set.strip_prefix_rules[0].id] : [],
    local.has_cors_rules ? [azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_rule_set.cors_rules[0].id] : []
  ))
  enabled = true

  # Use HTTP for internal backends (unless specifically HTTPS)
  forwarding_protocol = each.value.protocol == "Https" ? "HttpsOnly" : "HttpOnly"
  patterns_to_match   = distinct([each.value.path, trimsuffix(each.value.path, "/*")])
  supported_protocols = ["Http", "Https"]
}

toolUseResult.originalFile:
##
## Prod Environment - Main Configuration
## Author: Raul Peña (rpena@shokworks.io)
##
## Purpose: Orchestrates all infrastructure modules for the production environment
## Environment: Production
## VNet: 10.2.0.0/16
## Subnets: database (10.2.2.0/24), container-apps (10.2.3.0/24), legacy-apps (10.2.4.0/24), bastion (10.2.5.0/24)
##
## Modules & Resources Deployed:
## - Networking: VNet, subnets, NSGs
## - Storage: Storage accounts, Key Vault, Log Analytics
## - PostgreSQL: Flexible Server (Burstable B2s, 50GB Premium SSD) in database subnet
##


##=============================================================================
## CURRENT AZURE CONTEXT
##=============================================================================
## Get current subscription and tenant information for identity and RBAC configuration

data "azurerm_client_config" "current" {}



##=============================================================================
## RESOURCE GROUP
##=============================================================================
## Primary container for all production environment resources
## Resource naming convention: rg-intellxxdna-lexxi-prod
## Tags are managed by IaC and ignore manual changes

# Create Resource Group
resource "azurerm_resource_group" "rg" {

  name     = local.resource_group_name
  location = var.azure_region
  tags     = local.tags

  lifecycle {
    ignore_changes = [tags]
  }
}



##=============================================================================
## NETWORKING MODULE
##=============================================================================
## Creates Virtual Network and subnets for the production environment
## Configuration:
## - VNet: 10.2.0.0/16
## - Container-Apps subnet: 10.2.3.0/24 (for container deployments)
## - Legacy-Apps subnet: 10.2.4.0/24 (for traditional VMs/apps)
##
## Each subnet includes:
## - Network Security Group (NSG) for traffic control
## - Service endpoints for Azure services (SQL, Storage, Key Vault)
## - Default allow rule for internal VNet communication

# Networking Module
module "networking" {

  source = "../../modules/networking"

  resource_group_name = azurerm_resource_group.rg.name
  location            = var.azure_region
  environment         = var.environment
  resource_prefix     = local.resource_prefix

  vnet_address_space = var.vnet_address_space

  subnets = {

    database = {
      address_prefix = "10.2.2.0/24"
      description    = "Database private subnet"
    }
    container-apps = {
      address_prefix = "10.2.3.0/24"
      description    = "Container Apps private subnet"
    }
    legacy-apps = {
      address_prefix = "10.2.4.0/24"
      description    = "Legacy applications private subnet"
    }
    bastion = {
      address_prefix = "10.2.5.0/24"
      description    = "Bastion jump host subnet"
    }
  }

  tags = local.tags

  depends_on = [azurerm_resource_group.rg]
}



##=============================================================================
## STORAGE MODULE
##=============================================================================
## Manages storage infrastructure and monitoring
## Resources created:
## - Storage Account: General-purpose v2, LRS replication
## - Key Vault: Secure credential storage
## - Log Analytics Workspace: Central logging and monitoring
##
## Key Vault contains:
## - Secrets for database credentials (future use)
## - API keys and sensitive configuration
## - Connection strings and access tokens
##
## Log Analytics stores:
## - Bastion connection logs
## - Resource diagnostics and metrics
## - Application logs (when configured)
##
## Backup Settings:
## - Enabled: false (cost optimization for dev)
## - Retention: 7 days (minimal for development)

# Storage Module
module "storage" {

  source = "../../modules/storage"

  resource_group_name = module.networking.resource_group_name
  location            = var.azure_region
  environment         = var.environment
  resource_prefix     = local.resource_prefix

  enable_backup         = local.env_config.enable_backup
  backup_retention_days = local.env_config.backup_retention
  log_retention_days    = local.env_config.log_retention_days

  tags = local.tags

  depends_on = [module.networking]
}



##=============================================================================
## POSTGRESQL DATABASE MODULE
##=============================================================================
## PostgreSQL Flexible Server for production environment
## Re-enabled for Keycloak deployment
## Databases: intellxxdna (main app) + keycloak (Keycloak identity service)
##

# PostgreSQL Flexible Server
resource "azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server" "postgres" {

  name                = "${local.resource_prefix}-psql"
  resource_group_name = azurerm_resource_group.rg.name
  location            = var.azure_region

  # Deployment
  version                = "14"
  administrator_login    = var.postgres_admin_username
  administrator_password = var.postgres_admin_password

  # Compute & Storage
  sku_name   = "B_Standard_B2s"
  storage_mb = 65536 # 64 GiB in MB

  # Backup
  backup_retention_days        = 7
  geo_redundant_backup_enabled = false

  # Network
  delegated_subnet_id           = module.networking.database_subnet_id
  private_dns_zone_id           = azurerm_private_dns_zone.postgres.id
  public_network_access_enabled = false

  auto_grow_enabled = false

  tags = local.tags

  lifecycle {
    ignore_changes = [zone]
  }

  depends_on = [azurerm_private_dns_zone.postgres,
  azurerm_private_dns_zone_virtual_network_link.postgres]
}

# Private DNS Zone for PostgreSQL
resource "azurerm_private_dns_zone" "postgres" {

  name                = "${var.environment}.postgres.database.azure.com"
  resource_group_name = azurerm_resource_group.rg.name

  tags = local.tags
}

# Link Private DNS Zone to VNet
resource "azurerm_private_dns_zone_virtual_network_link" "postgres" {

  name                  = "${local.resource_prefix}-postgres-vnet-link"
  resource_group_name   = azurerm_resource_group.rg.name
  private_dns_zone_name = azurerm_private_dns_zone.postgres.name
  virtual_network_id    = module.networking.vnet_id

  depends_on = [azurerm_private_dns_zone.postgres]
}

# Database for the application
resource "azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server_database" "app_database" {

  name      = "intellxxdna"
  server_id = azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres.id
  charset   = "UTF8"
  collation = "en_US.utf8"

  depends_on = [azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres]
}

# Database for Keycloak
resource "azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server_database" "keycloak_database" {

  name      = "keycloak"
  server_id = azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres.id
  charset   = "UTF8"
  collation = "en_US.utf8"

  depends_on = [azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres]
}


##=============================================================================
## KEY VAULT SECRETS - DATABASE CREDENTIALS
##=============================================================================
## PostgreSQL connection parameters stored securely in Key Vault
## Re-enabled for Keycloak and application database access
##=============================================================================

# PostgreSQL Host
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "postgres_host" {
  name         = "postgres-host"
  value        = azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres.fqdn
  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id

  depends_on = [azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres]
}

# PostgreSQL Port
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "postgres_port" {
  name         = "postgres-port"
  value        = "5432"
  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id
}

# PostgreSQL Database Name
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "lexxi_postgres_db" {
  name         = "lexxi-postgres-db"
  value        = "intellxxdna"
  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id
}

# PostgreSQL Admin Username
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "postgres_user" {
  name         = "postgres-user"
  value        = var.postgres_admin_username
  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id
}

# PostgreSQL Admin Password
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "postgres_password" {
  name         = "postgres-password"
  value        = var.postgres_admin_password
  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id
}

# RD PostgreSQL URL (Full connection string)
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "intellxxdna_postgres_url" {
  name         = "rd-postgres-url"
  value        = "postgresql+asyncpg://${var.intellxxdna_admin_username}:${urlencode(var.intellxxdna_admin_password)}@${azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres.fqdn}:5432/intellxxdna"
  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id

  depends_on = [azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres]
}

# Disable SSL requirement to match dev environment behavior
resource "azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server_configuration" "require_secure_transport" {
  name      = "require_secure_transport"
  server_id = azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres.id
  value     = "off"

  depends_on = [azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres]
}

# PostgreSQL Firewall Rule - Allow container-apps subnet
resource "azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server_firewall_rule" "container_apps" {

  name             = "AllowContainerAppsSubnet"
  server_id        = azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres.id
  start_ip_address = "10.2.3.0"
  end_ip_address   = "10.2.3.255"

  depends_on = [azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres]
}

# PostgreSQL Firewall Rule - Allow legacy-apps subnet
resource "azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server_firewall_rule" "legacy_apps" {

  name             = "AllowLegacyAppsSubnet"
  server_id        = azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres.id
  start_ip_address = "10.2.4.0"
  end_ip_address   = "10.2.4.255"

  depends_on = [azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres]
}



##=============================================================================
## CONTAINER APPS ENVIRONMENT & APPLICATION
##=============================================================================
## Azure Container Apps for containerized workloads
## Configuration:
## - Plan: Consumption (pay-per-use, auto-scaling)
## - Environment: Connected to container-apps subnet (10.2.3.0/24)
## - Image: lexxi from shared ACR (ixdnashared.azurecr.io)
## - Resources: 1 vCPU, 2GB memory
## - Replicas: Minimum 1, Maximum 3
##
## The Container Apps Environment integrates with the VNet for
## secure, isolated container deployments within the private network

# Container Apps Environment connected to VNet
resource "azurerm_container_app_environment" "env" {

  name                           = "${local.resource_prefix}-cae"
  location                       = var.azure_region
  resource_group_name            = azurerm_resource_group.rg.name
  log_analytics_workspace_id     = module.storage.log_analytics_workspace_id
  infrastructure_subnet_id       = module.networking.container_apps_subnet_id
  internal_load_balancer_enabled = false

  tags = local.tags

  depends_on = [module.networking, module.storage]

  lifecycle {
    ignore_changes = [tags, infrastructure_resource_group_name]
  }
}



#
# Container App - RD
#

# RD Swagger user name.
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "rd_swagger_username" {
  name  = "rd-swagger-username"
  value = var.rd_swagger_username

  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id

  depends_on = [module.storage]
}

# RD Swagger user password.
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "rd_swagger_pw" {
  name  = "rd-swagger-pw"
  value = var.rd_swagger_pw

  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id

  depends_on = [module.storage]
}

# RD environment.
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "rd_environment" {
  name  = "rd-environment"
  value = var.rd_environment

  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id

  depends_on = [module.storage]
}

# RD JWT_SECRET_KEY.
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "rd_jwt_secret_key" {
  name  = "rd-jwt-secret-key"
  value = var.rd_jwt_secret_key

  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id

  depends_on = [module.storage]
}

# RD CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS.
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "rd_cors_allow_origins" {
  name  = "rd-cors-allow-origins"
  value = var.rd_cors_allow_origins

  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id

  depends_on = [module.storage]
}

# RD ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES.
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "rd_access_token_expire_minutes" {
  name  = "rd-access-token-expire-minutes"
  value = var.rd_access_token_expire_minutes

  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id

  depends_on = [module.storage]
}

# RD Sentry DSN.
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "rd_sentry_dsn" {
  name  = "rd-sentry-dsn"
  value = var.rd_sentry_dsn

  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id

  depends_on = [module.storage]
}

# RD Container app.
resource "azurerm_container_app" "rd" {

  name                         = "${local.resource_prefix}-rd"
  container_app_environment_id = azurerm_container_app_environment.env.id
  resource_group_name          = azurerm_resource_group.rg.name
  revision_mode                = "Single"

  # Enable managed identity for Key Vault secret access
  identity {
    type = "SystemAssigned"
  }

  # Enable ingress for public access
  ingress {

    allow_insecure_connections = true
    external_enabled           = true
    target_port                = 3000
    transport                  = "http"

    dynamic "ip_security_restriction" {
      for_each = { for idx, ip in local.front_door_backend_ips : "fd${idx}" => ip }
      content {
        name             = ip_security_restriction.key
        action           = "Allow"
        ip_address_range = ip_security_restriction.value
        description      = "Azure Front Door backend IP"
      }
    }

    traffic_weight {
      latest_revision = true
      percentage      = 100
    }
  }

  template {

    container {

      name   = "rd"
      image  = "${var.shared_container_registry_login_server}/${var.container_rd_app_image_name}:dev-latest"
      cpu    = var.container_app_cpu
      memory = var.container_app_memory

      # Database credentials from Key Vault
      # Note: Container Apps doesn't support @Microsoft.KeyVault() syntax
      # We fetch actual values from Key Vault via Terraform at apply time
      env {
        name  = "POSTGRES_URL"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.intellxxdna_postgres_url.value
      }

      env {
        name  = "SWAGGER_USERNAME"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.rd_swagger_username.value
      }

      env {
        name  = "SWAGGER_PASSWORD"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.rd_swagger_pw.value
      }

      env {
        name  = "ENVIRONMENT"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.rd_environment.value
      }

      env {
        name  = "JWT_SECRET_KEY"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.rd_jwt_secret_key.value
      }

      env {
        name  = "CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.rd_cors_allow_origins.value
      }

      env {
        name  = "ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.rd_access_token_expire_minutes.value
      }

      env {
        name  = "SENTRY_DSN"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.rd_sentry_dsn.value
      }
    }

    min_replicas = var.container_app_min_replicas
    max_replicas = var.container_app_max_replicas
  }

  registry {
    server               = var.shared_container_registry_login_server
    username             = var.shared_container_registry_username
    password_secret_name = "acr-password"
  }

  secret {
    name  = "acr-password"
    value = var.shared_container_registry_password
  }

  tags = local.tags

  lifecycle {
    ignore_changes = [workload_profile_name]
  }

  depends_on = [
    azurerm_container_app_environment.env,
    azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres,
    azurerm_key_vault_secret.intellxxdna_postgres_url
    # module.redis
  ]
}



#
# Container App - Lexxi
#

# Lexxi OpenAI token
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "lexxi_openai_token" {
  name  = "lexxi-openai-token"
  value = var.lexxi_openai_token

  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id

  depends_on = [module.storage]
}

# Lexxi CORS allow origins
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "lexxi_cors_allow_origins" {
  name  = "lexxi-cors-allow-origins"
  value = var.lexxi_cors_allow_origins

  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id

  depends_on = [module.storage]
}

# Lexxi Sentry DSN.
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "lexxi_sentry_dsn" {
  name  = "lexxi-sentry-dsn"
  value = var.lexxi_sentry_dsn

  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id

  depends_on = [module.storage]
}

# Lexxi Container app.
resource "azurerm_container_app" "lexxi" {

  name                         = "${local.resource_prefix}-lexxi"
  container_app_environment_id = azurerm_container_app_environment.env.id
  resource_group_name          = azurerm_resource_group.rg.name
  revision_mode                = "Single"

  # Enable managed identity for Key Vault secret access
  identity {
    type = "SystemAssigned"
  }

  # Enable ingress for public access
  ingress {

    allow_insecure_connections = true
    external_enabled           = true
    target_port                = 3000
    transport                  = "http"

    dynamic "ip_security_restriction" {
      for_each = { for idx, ip in local.front_door_backend_ips : "fd${idx}" => ip }
      content {
        name             = ip_security_restriction.key
        action           = "Allow"
        ip_address_range = ip_security_restriction.value
        description      = "Azure Front Door backend IP"
      }
    }

    traffic_weight {
      latest_revision = true
      percentage      = 100
    }
  }

  template {

    container {

      name   = "lexxi"
      image  = "${var.shared_container_registry_login_server}/${var.container_lexxi_app_image_name}:dev-latest"
      cpu    = var.container_app_cpu
      memory = var.container_app_memory

      # Database credentials from Key Vault
      # Note: Container Apps doesn't support @Microsoft.KeyVault() syntax
      # We fetch actual values from Key Vault via Terraform at apply time
      env {
        name  = "POSTGRES_URL"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.intellxxdna_postgres_url.value
      }

      env {
        name  = "OPENAI_API_KEY"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.lexxi_openai_token.value
      }

      env {
        name  = "CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.lexxi_cors_allow_origins.value
      }

      env {
        name  = "SENTRY_DSN"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.lexxi_sentry_dsn.value
      }
    }

    min_replicas = var.container_app_min_replicas
    max_replicas = var.container_app_max_replicas
  }

  registry {
    server               = var.shared_container_registry_login_server
    username             = var.shared_container_registry_username
    password_secret_name = "acr-password"
  }

  secret {
    name  = "acr-password"
    value = var.shared_container_registry_password
  }

  # NOTE: Lexxi container app uses Terraform-fetched values from Key Vault
  # (see env blocks above), so no additional secret blocks needed here

  tags = local.tags

  lifecycle {
    ignore_changes = [workload_profile_name]
  }

  depends_on = [
    azurerm_container_app_environment.env,
    azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres,
    azurerm_key_vault_secret.postgres_host,
    azurerm_key_vault_secret.postgres_port,
    azurerm_key_vault_secret.lexxi_postgres_db,
    azurerm_key_vault_secret.postgres_user,
    azurerm_key_vault_secret.postgres_password
    # module.redis
  ]
}



##=============================================================================
## KEYCLOAK CONTAINER APP
##=============================================================================
## Keycloak Identity and Access Management (IAM)
## Configuration:
## - Image: keycloak/keycloak:latest (official Docker image)
## - Database: PostgreSQL database "keycloak"
## - Admin: Configured via environment variables
## - Features: OIDC/OAuth2, LDAP/AD integration, realms, clients
## - Access: Public via Front Door + direct
## - Port: 8080
##

##=============================================================================
## KEY VAULT SECRETS - KEYCLOAK CONFIGURATION
##=============================================================================
## Keycloak-specific configuration stored securely in Key Vault
## These values are referenced by the Keycloak container app via Key Vault URIs
##=============================================================================

# Keycloak Database URL (JDBC connection string)
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "keycloak_db_url" {
  name  = "keycloak-db-url"
  value = "jdbc:postgresql://${azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres.fqdn}/keycloak"

  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id

  depends_on = [azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres]
}

# Keycloak Database Username
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "keycloak_db_username" {
  name         = "keycloak-db-username"
  value        = var.postgres_admin_username
  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id
}

# Keycloak Database Password
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "keycloak_db_password" {
  name         = "keycloak-db-password"
  value        = var.postgres_admin_password
  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id
}

# Keycloak Admin Username
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "keycloak_admin" {
  name         = "keycloak-admin"
  value        = var.keycloak_admin_username
  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id
}

# Keycloak Admin Password
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "keycloak_admin_password" {
  name         = "keycloak-admin-password"
  value        = var.keycloak_admin_password
  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id
}

# Keycloak Container App
resource "azurerm_container_app" "keycloak" {

  name                         = "${local.resource_prefix}-auth"
  container_app_environment_id = azurerm_container_app_environment.env.id
  resource_group_name          = azurerm_resource_group.rg.name
  revision_mode                = "Single"

  # Enable managed identity for Key Vault secret access
  identity {
    type = "SystemAssigned"
  }

  # Enable ingress for public access
  # Note: Multiple revisions allow zero-downtime deployments with traffic splitting
  # GitHub Actions will manage gradual traffic shift from old revision (90%) to new revision (10%)
  ingress {

    allow_insecure_connections = true
    external_enabled           = true
    target_port                = 8080
    transport                  = "http"

    dynamic "ip_security_restriction" {
      for_each = { for idx, ip in local.front_door_backend_ips : "fd${idx}" => ip }
      content {
        name             = ip_security_restriction.key
        action           = "Allow"
        ip_address_range = ip_security_restriction.value
        description      = "Azure Front Door backend IP"
      }
    }

    # B.1 Zero-Downtime Deployment Strategy with Traffic Splitting
    # Allows gradual rollout: 10% new, 90% old → 25/75 → 50/50 → 75/25 → 100% new
    # Deployment script (bin/deploy-keycloak-zero-downtime.sh) dynamically manages traffic weights
    # and updates revision references as new revisions are created by Azure

    traffic_weight {
      latest_revision = true
      percentage      = 100 # Terraform baseline: 100% on latest
      # Deployment script modifies this to 10% for zero-downtime rollout
    }
  }

  template {

    container {

      name   = "keycloak"
      image  = "keycloak/keycloak:latest"
      cpu    = 1.0
      memory = "2Gi"

      # Start Keycloak in production mode
      command = ["/opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh", "start"]

      # Database configuration - values from Key Vault secrets
      # Note: References the secret block definitions below for Key Vault integration

      # Database type.
      env {
        name  = "KC_DB"
        value = "postgres"
      }

      # Database url, username, and password from Key Vault secrets
      env {
        name  = "KC_DB_URL"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.keycloak_db_url.value
      }

      env {
        name  = "KC_DB_USERNAME"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.keycloak_db_username.value
      }

      env {
        name  = "KC_DB_PASSWORD"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.keycloak_db_password.value
      }

      # Admin credentials - values from Key Vault secrets

      env {
        name  = "KEYCLOAK_ADMIN"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.keycloak_admin.value
      }

      env {
        name  = "KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.keycloak_admin_password.value
      }

      # Note: Keycloak has strict type validation for configuration parameters,
      # so boolean and enum values must be direct values, not Key Vault references

      env {
        name  = "KC_HOSTNAME_STRICT"
        value = "false"
      }

      env {
        name  = "KC_HTTP_ENABLED"
        value = "true"
      }

      env {
        name  = "KC_PROXY"
        value = "edge"
      }

      env {
        name  = "KC_PROXY_HEADERS"
        value = "xforwarded"
      }

      env {
        name  = "KC_SCHEME"
        value = "https"
      }

      env {
        name  = "KC_HTTP_RELATIVE_PATH"
        value = "/auth"
      }

      env {
        name  = "KC_SSL_REQUIRED"
        value = "none"
      }
    }

    min_replicas = 1
    max_replicas = 2
  }

  tags = local.tags

  depends_on = [
    azurerm_container_app_environment.env,
    azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server_database.keycloak_database,
    azurerm_key_vault_secret.keycloak_db_url,
    azurerm_key_vault_secret.keycloak_db_username,
    azurerm_key_vault_secret.keycloak_db_password,
    azurerm_key_vault_secret.keycloak_admin,
    azurerm_key_vault_secret.keycloak_admin_password
  ]
}

##=============================================================================
## KEY VAULT ACCESS POLICY - KEYCLOAK
##=============================================================================
## Grant Keycloak container app managed identity permissions to read secrets
## from Key Vault for environment variable substitution
## NOTE: This resource is commented out initially and must be applied in a
## second step after the Keycloak container app (and its identity) is created.
## To apply: Uncomment this block and run terraform apply again.
##=============================================================================

resource "azurerm_key_vault_access_policy" "keycloak" {

  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id
  tenant_id    = data.azurerm_client_config.current.tenant_id
  object_id    = azurerm_container_app.keycloak.identity[0].principal_id

  secret_permissions = ["Get", "List"]

  depends_on = [azurerm_container_app.keycloak]
}



##=============================================================================
## AZURE FRONT DOOR MODULE
##=============================================================================
## Provides global public access with managed HTTPS (*.azurefd.net certificate)
## Features:
## - Automatic HTTPS certificate management (no renewal needed)
## - WAF protection with OWASP ModSecurity rules
## - Global CDN with DDoS protection (layers 3-7)
## - HTTP → HTTPS automatic redirect
## - Health probes for backend monitoring
## Status: Replaces Application Gateway for lower cost ($6/month vs $32/month) and simplicity
##

module "front_door" {

  source = "../../modules/front-door"

  resource_group_name = azurerm_resource_group.rg.name
  environment         = var.environment
  resource_prefix     = local.resource_prefix
  tags                = local.tags

  # Front Door Configuration
  front_door_sku          = var.front_door_sku
  custom_domain_host_name = "api.ixxdna.com"

  # Backend Configuration - Multiple backends for Lexxi, RD, and Keycloak with path-based routing
  # NOTE: Route Keycloak over HTTPS to bypass HTTP-only proxy header issues
  backends = [
    {
      name              = "lexxi"
      fqdn              = azurerm_container_app.lexxi.ingress[0].fqdn
      port              = 80
      protocol          = "Http"
      path              = "/lexxi/*"
      health_path       = "/"
      strip_path_prefix = true
    },
    {
      name              = "rd"
      fqdn              = azurerm_container_app.rd.ingress[0].fqdn
      port              = 80
      protocol          = "Http"
      path              = "/rd/*"
      health_path       = "/"
      strip_path_prefix = true
    },
    {
      name             = "keycloak"
      fqdn             = azurerm_container_app.keycloak.ingress[0].fqdn
      port             = 443
      protocol         = "Https"
      path             = "/auth/*"
      health_path      = "/auth/realms/master/.well-known/openid-configuration"
      session_affinity = true
    }
  ]

  # WAF Configuration
  enable_waf = var.enable_front_door_waf
  waf_mode   = var.front_door_waf_mode

  depends_on = [
    azurerm_container_app.lexxi,
    azurerm_container_app.rd,
    azurerm_container_app.keycloak,
    module.networking
  ]
}



##=============================================================================
## STATIC WEB APP MODULE (lexxi)
##=============================================================================
## Azure Static Web App for Angular frontend deployment
## Features:
## - Free tier (no cost)
## - GitHub Actions CI/CD integration
## - Managed HTTPS certificate (*.azurestaticapps.net)
## - Global CDN distribution
## - API proxy to backend via staticwebapp.config.json
##
## Deployment:
## - Automatically deployed from main branch
## - Build command: ng build
## - Build output: dist/
## - GitHub Enterprise authentication via PAT token
##
## Access:
## - Frontend: https://<app-name>.azurestaticapps.net
## - API: Proxied through Static Web App to Front Door endpoint
##

module "static_web_app_lexxi" {

  source = "../../modules/static-web-app"

  resource_group_name = azurerm_resource_group.rg.name
  location            = var.azure_region
  environment         = var.environment
  resource_prefix     = local.resource_prefix
  app_name            = "lexxis"

  # GitHub Configuration
  github_repo_url = var.lexxi_github_repo_url
  github_token    = var.github_token
  github_branch   = var.lexxi_github_branch

  # Build Configuration
  app_location    = var.swa_app_location
  output_location = var.swa_output_location
  build_command   = var.swa_build_command

  # API Configuration
  api_endpoint = module.front_door.endpoint_fqdn
  api_key      = ""

  sku_tier = "Free"
  sku_size = "Free"
  tags     = local.tags

  depends_on = [
    module.front_door,
    azurerm_container_app.lexxi
  ]
}

## STATIC WEB APP CUSTOM DOMAIN for lexxi
resource "azurerm_static_web_app_custom_domain" "lexxi_prod" {

  count             = var.enable_custom_domain_to_lexxi ? 1 : 0
  static_web_app_id = module.static_web_app_lexxi.static_web_app_id
  domain_name       = "lexxi.ixxdna.com"
  validation_type   = "cname-delegation"
}



##=============================================================================
## STATIC WEB APP MODULE (rd)
##=============================================================================
## Azure Static Web App for Angular frontend deployment
## Features:
## - Free tier (no cost)
## - GitHub Actions CI/CD integration
## - Managed HTTPS certificate (*.azurestaticapps.net)
## - Global CDN distribution
## - API proxy to backend via staticwebapp.config.json
##
## Deployment:
## - Automatically deployed from main branch
## - Build command: ng build
## - Build output: dist/
## - GitHub Enterprise authentication via PAT token
##
## Access:
## - Frontend: https://<app-name>.azurestaticapps.net
## - API: Proxied through Static Web App to Front Door endpoint
##

module "static_web_app_rd" {

  source = "../../modules/static-web-app"

  resource_group_name = azurerm_resource_group.rg.name
  location            = var.azure_region
  environment         = var.environment
  resource_prefix     = local.resource_prefix
  app_name            = "rd"

  # GitHub Configuration
  github_repo_url = var.rd_github_repo_url
  github_token    = var.github_token
  github_branch   = var.rd_github_branch

  # Build Configuration
  app_location    = var.swa_app_location
  output_location = var.swa_output_location
  build_command   = var.swa_build_command

  # API Configuration
  api_endpoint = module.front_door.endpoint_fqdn
  api_key      = ""

  sku_tier = "Free"
  sku_size = "Free"
  tags     = local.tags

  depends_on = [
    module.front_door,
    azurerm_container_app.rd
  ]
}

## STATIC WEB APP CUSTOM DOMAIN for rnd
resource "azurerm_static_web_app_custom_domain" "rnd_prod" {

  count             = var.enable_custom_domain_to_rd ? 1 : 0
  static_web_app_id = module.static_web_app_rd.static_web_app_id
  domain_name       = "rnd.ixxdna.com"
  validation_type   = "cname-delegation"
}
